title: Local spectral theory and spectral inclusions
author_string: Laursen, Kjeld B., Neumann, Michael M.
abstract: Suppose thatTandSare continuous linear operators on complex Banach spacesXandY, respectively, and thatAis a non-zero continuous linear mapping fromXtoY. IfAintertwinesTandSin the sense thatSA=AT, then a classical result due to Rosenblum implies that the spectra σ(T) and σ(S) must overlap, see [12]. Actually, Davis and Rosenthal [5]have shown that the surjectivity spectrum σsu(T) will meet the approximate point spectrum σap(S) in this case (terms to be denned below). Further information about the relations between the two spectra and their finer structure becomes available when the intertwinerAis injective or has dense range, see [9], [12], [13].
